title: BaseRT targets faster private LLM inference on Apple silicon
friendlyTitle: BaseRT runs private AI models faster on Macs
summary: Base Compute launched BaseRT, a local model runtime for Apple silicon with support for Qwen, Llama, Gemma, Mistral, Phi, and Nomic models. On an M5 Pro, the company reports up to 33% faster decoding and substantially faster prompt processing than selected MLX and llama.cpp configurations; it also provides a local server workflow for coding agents.
friendlySummary: BaseRT lets people run several popular AI models on Apple computers without sending data to a cloud service. Its maker reports faster results than common local tools in selected tests.
whyItMatters: Faster local inference can cut per-token costs and keep sensitive code or documents on the user’s machine. The benchmark claims are vendor-supplied and hardware-dependent, so teams should reproduce them with their own models and Macs before changing stacks.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Local AI can save API money and protect private data. Test it on your own Mac first because speed can change by model and chip.
